topic.what: First direct observation of human gastric physiology through a permanent gastric fistula, proving digestion is chemical not mechanical.
topic.how: 238 controlled experiments observing digestion in vivo through the fistula wound of Alexis St. Martin over 8+ years.
topic.why_matters: Transformed gastric disease from humoral abstraction to measurable physiology — the founding work of modern gastroenterology.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of controlled gastric experiments performed
characteristics.vs_baseline: First systematic in vivo gastric experiments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree to which findings overturned existing understanding
characteristics.value: Replaced 2000-year humoral model with chemical digestion model

good_for: Understanding origins of modern gastric physiology
Historical context for acid-centric gastric disease models
requires: Historical context for ethical standards of the era

novelty: First proof that digestion is primarily chemical (acid-based), not mechanical
First observation that emotions affect gastric function — anticipating psychogastroenterology
First description of gastric mucosal appearance under different physiological states
First in vivo demonstration of gastric acid composition
open_problems: Ethical concerns by modern standards (coercive research relationship with St. Martin)
Pre-dates germ theory — no awareness of microbial factors
